name: Family Services Center, Inc.
description_short: Non-profit counseling and family services organization offering free and low-cost support for counseling, housing assistance, substance abuse treatment, and financial empowerment in the Huntsville area.
description_long:
Family Services Center, Inc. (FSC) is an established non-profit organization based in Huntsville, Alabama, with over 100 years of combined professional experience among its staff. The organization is built on a foundation of providing therapeutic and supportive services to individuals and families navigating life's challenges, emphasizing respect and trust in all client relationships. FSC operates as a HUD-aligned community services provider, offering comprehensive support across multiple life domains without requiring users to navigate the consumer lending market.

FSC provides a broad spectrum of free and subsidized services including individual and family counseling, housing and financial empowerment programs, substance abuse treatment and recovery support, family strengthening initiatives, behavioral education, and specialized support for crime victims and homicide survivors. Their Housing & Financial Empowerment program specifically helps clients identify and meet financial goals to support employment and housing stability. The organization accepts multiple health insurance plans and functions as a service provider for Employee Assistance Programs (EAPs) through local employers, many of which offer no-cost counseling to employees and their families.

What distinguishes FSC is its holistic, trauma-informed approach that extends beyond basic financial counseling to address underlying behavioral, relational, and substance-use factors affecting financial stability. The organization's depth of services—spanning counseling, family support, workforce development, and crisis support for crime victims—creates a comprehensive safety net. Their integration with employer EAPs and health insurance acceptance makes services accessible to working individuals who might not identify as needing traditional financial help. The organization's commitment to transparency is evident through publicly available IRS Form 990 filings and community needs assessments.

The primary limitation is geographic restriction to the Huntsville, Alabama area via their listed phone number (256-551-1610) and local service base. While their website indicates services availability, specific details about eligibility requirements, waitlists, or service capacity are not provided online. For consumers seeking remote financial counseling or those outside the service area, FSC would not be accessible. Additionally, while they offer financial empowerment services, the website does not detail whether they provide credit repair, credit building, or debt negotiation—their focus appears primarily on stability and life skills rather than credit score improvement.
pros: Over 100 years of combined professional experience among staff, indicating established expertise and organizational stability
Comprehensive service portfolio addressing root causes of financial instability including substance abuse, behavioral issues, and family dysfunction
Multiple access pathways including employer EAP programs with no-cost counseling options and acceptance of health insurance plans
Specialized support for vulnerable populations including homicide survivors, crime victims, and families experiencing domestic challenges
Transparent organization with published IRS Form 990, annual reports, and community needs assessments available
Housing & Financial Empowerment program directly targets employment and housing stability, addressing foundational economic security
Emphasis on therapeutic relationship and respect within all client interactions, reducing stigma of seeking help
cons: Geographic limitation to Huntsville, Alabama area with no indication of remote services or multi-state availability
Website lacks specific information about eligibility requirements, service capacity, wait times, or intake procedures
No mention of credit repair, credit building tools, or credit score improvement services despite 'financial empowerment' branding
Limited details about substance abuse program specifics, treatment modalities, or success rates
No pricing information provided for uninsured clients or those without EAP access, creating uncertainty about actual cost
best_for: Individuals seeking counseling and family support services integrated with financial stability planning in Huntsville, Alabama
Employees with access to EAPs seeking free or subsidized counseling for personal, family, or substance-use challenges
Families experiencing housing instability who need both financial coaching and therapeutic support for underlying behavioral or relational issues
Crime victims and homicide survivors requiring grief counseling and support services alongside practical assistance
diagnosis: FSC is best for Huntsville-area residents seeking integrated counseling and financial stability support rooted in understanding life's underlying challenges rather than quick-fix credit solutions. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to Alabama's Huntsville region and lack of remote service options, making the organization inaccessible to consumers outside this area or those requiring virtual-only support.
services: Housing & Financial Empowerment program (financial goal identification and planning)
Substance Abuse treatment and recovery support
Family Support programs including parenting support
Behavioral Education and behavior management support
Homicide Survivors & Crime Victims counseling and grief support
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 520 Madison St Se, Suite B, Huntsville, AL 35801
